decision.title: Python 3.11+ as implementation language
decision.summary: Python 3.11+ chosen. Test mandates Python; 3.11 has best async perf + ergonomics for realtime audio. asyncio + dataclasses + type hints give clean structure. (source: test constraint)
decision.impact: All code ships as Python; venv-based isolation; pip deps only

decision.title: Deepgram Python SDK + websocket streaming
decision.summary: Deepgram SDK v3+ with streaming websocket. Mandated by test. interim_results=True + punctuate=True give word-level timestamps with <300ms latency. (source: test step 1)
decision.impact: Mic audio streams to Deepgram; word timestamps drive char interpolation

decision.title: Groq as LLM provider (llama-3.1-8b-instant primary)
decision.summary: Groq chosen for LLM. Fastest first-token among Groq/Fireworks/Cerebras/OpenRouter (~200 tok/s). Primary model: llama-3.1-8b-instant. Fallback: llama-3.3-70b-versatile or Cerebras if rate-limited. (source: test step 4, operator choice)

decision.title: ElevenLabs streaming TTS with text_to_speech.with_timestamps
decision.summary: ElevenLabs chosen for TTS. text_to_speech.with_timestamps endpoint provides char-level timestamps natively, avoiding client-side derivation. Voice: Rachel or Adam (pre-made, no extra setup). (source: test step 5-6)
decision.impact: TTS char timestamps exact not interpolated; playback via sounddevice

decision.title: sounddevice for mic capture and playback
decision.summary: sounddevice library for microphone capture and audio playback. Simpler than pyaudio on Windows, supports numpy buffers directly, less native-dep friction. (source: platform constraint + test step 1)
decision.impact: Mic capture and TTS playback both use sounddevice; numpy dependency

decision.title: Single append-only JSONL data store for all chars
decision.summary: Unified char data store: Python list of dataclasses (char, start_time, end_time, notes) with JSONL persistence on each batch flush. Simple, debuggable, matches test schema exactly. notes field carries source tag (user|bot) and derivation method. (source: test steps 2+6)
decision.impact: Single file output; easy to inspect and evaluate; no DB dependency

decision.title: Crosstalk strategy deferred until tarzain/crosstalk reviewed
decision.summary: Part 2 crosstalk implementation approach deferred until operator reads https://github.com/tarzain/crosstalk. Likely involves VAD + early-LLM-start + generation cancellation. Capture actual approach in follow-up decision after repo review. (source: test Part 2)
decision.impact: Phase 02 planning blocked on repo read; unblocked in ~15 min of study

decision.title: Repo strategy: public GitHub repo shared by URL or collaborator
decision.summary: Create new public GitHub repo (B2Gdevs/conduit-proj or similar) at end of session. Share with RioPopper either by adding as collaborator via GitHub UI or making repo public and sharing URL. Decide at session end based on time remaining. (source: test deliverable)
decision.impact: No repo action before code is working; sharing is last step

decision.title: Textual TUI as conduit_proj operator UI.
decision.summary: Textual TUI as conduit_proj operator UI. Single Textual app wires audio capture loop + Deepgram streaming client + char-timeline store + LLM client + TTS client. Layout: left pane 'You said' (user live transcript), right pane 'LLM said' (bot response stream), bottom DataTable showing the char-timeline (char, start, end, notes) as it appends, status bar (mic level, Deepgram status, Groq latency, ElevenLabs status). Keeps everything in Python. Avoids web-frontend overhead. Deps: textual textual-dev watchfiles sounddevice deepgram-sdk groq elevenlabs python-dotenv numpy.

decision.title: Reuse Textual patterns from ~/Documents/slm_learning/scri...
decision.summary: Reuse Textual patterns from ~/Documents/slm_learning/scripts/learning_tui/ (17 modules, 2355 LOC). Highest-value: chat_screen.py (519 LOC, chat-first 2-panel layout with composer + output rail), local_speech.py (168 LOC, sounddevice mic capture directly portable to Deepgram websocket by swapping backend), app.py + app_banner.py (~70 LOC), visual_context.py (202 LOC, optional VCS dev mode Alt+I overlay), slash_commands.py (119 LOC, optional /reset /save /export). DO NOT copy verbatim — read patterns + reimplement against Deepgram+Groq+ElevenLabs stack. Cite slm_learning module names in inline comments.

decision.title: Switch LLM provider from Groq → OpenRouter (operator dire...
decision.summary: Switch LLM provider from Groq → OpenRouter (operator direction 2026-05-15). OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible, routes to any provider (Groq/Anthropic/OpenAI/etc.) via 'provider/model' strings. Default model: meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct (balanced quality + speed). Client: openai Python SDK with base_url='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1' and OPENROUTER_API_KEY in Authorization. Per memory feedback_no_ai_sdk_use_openai_compatible.md NEVER use ai/@ai-sdk/* packages. Fallback chain: OpenRouter → OpenAI direct (key already copied from monorepo) → Groq (if key set). Supersedes CONDUIT-PROJ-D-003 which picked Groq as primary.

decision.title: Crosstalk impl approach based on tarzain/crosstalk (commi...
decision.summary: Crosstalk impl approach based on tarzain/crosstalk (commit 327b2da). Silence-window algorithm: speculative LLM at 250ms post-last-is_final word, settle+commit at 600ms. Cancellation via asyncio.Task.cancel() — clean, no buffered audio loss. MIN_WORDS=3 guard prevents fragment speculation. No new VAD library — Deepgram is_final events are sufficient VAD signal. Thresholds are env-configurable via CROSSTALK_SPECULATIVE_THRESHOLD_MS / CROSSTALK_SETTLED_THRESHOLD_MS / CROSSTALK_MIN_WORDS.

decision.title: Free-tier ElevenLabs: eleven_flash_v2_5 + mp3_44100_128 + premade Sarah voice
decision.summary: ElevenLabs TTS on a free-tier account requires three concrete choices. (1) Model eleven_monolingual_v1 was deprecated on the free tier mid-2026; switch to eleven_flash_v2_5. (2) Output format pcm_44100 is Pro-tier-only; switch to mp3_44100_128 and decode via miniaudio before handing PCM to sounddevice. (3) Library voices (Rachel and similar) require a paid plan when called via API; default voice swapped to Sarah (EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL), a premade voice attached to the account that works on free tier. SDK response field is audio_base_64 with an underscore, not audio_base64 — easy footgun.
decision.impact: Unblocks audio output on any account without a paid plan. miniaudio adds a small native dependency but is pure pip-installable and works on Win/macOS/Linux.

decision.title: Barge-in via Deepgram partial-char threshold, not VAD
decision.summary: User-initiated barge-in is gated by BARGE_IN_PARTIAL_CHARS — the minimum number of characters in a Deepgram interim transcript that triggers sounddevice.stop() while TTS is playing. Default 3. Chose this over an RMS/VAD energy threshold because (a) Deepgram is already doing high-quality VAD upstream, (b) char count is hardware-independent (mic sensitivity, distance, room noise vary), (c) the same threshold doubles as a confidence signal that real speech is happening, not just a cough.
decision.impact: Predictable barge behavior across hardware. Tunable by the operator. The 'how chaotic two-way can get' demo behavior is preserved when threshold is low; raising it suppresses noise.

decision.title: Echo guard via bot-text word-set membership, no AEC
decision.summary: The mic picks up the bot's TTS through the speakers (no acoustic echo cancellation in the stack). Without filtering, the bot transcribes itself, which (a) tripped barge-in mid-sentence and (b) made the bot reply to itself in a feedback loop. The guard normalizes both the bot's outgoing text and each incoming Deepgram partial/final (lowercase, strip punctuation, collapse whitespace) and classifies the partial as echo only when EVERY word in it appears in the bot's text. The presence of even one novel word ('stop', 'wait', a name) means real user speech — barge fires. Guard stays armed for 250ms after sd.play() returns to absorb late finals; longer tails started swallowing the next legitimate user turn.
decision.impact: Restores reliable human barge-in. Trade-off: if the user happens to say only words the bot is already using, barge is suppressed — accepted as a rare edge case. A future AEC step (e.g., echo subtraction using TTS char timeline) would remove the trade-off.

decision.title: Loop prevention via hard time gate on Crosstalk finals during TTS+cooldown, not text heuristics
decision.summary: The text-content echo heuristic (word-set membership against the bot's outgoing text) is structurally insufficient. Deepgram is a probabilistic model and occasionally inserts a hallucinated word (cough/breath/room noise mis-transcribed) which makes the partial appear to contain novel user speech. That single novel word slipped the heuristic, the 'echo' transcript entered Crosstalk, and the bot replied to itself in a self-feedback loop. Replace the heuristic with a hard time-based gate: while TTS is playing AND for POST_TTS_COOLDOWN_S (default 0.6s) afterward, Deepgram is_final events are unconditionally dropped before reaching Crosstalk. No text-based escape hatch — if the bot is speaking, no LLM cycle can begin. Barge-in opens the gate immediately so the user's intentional cut is captured in the next final without waiting out the cooldown. The text-based echo check is retained on the partial→barge path so the bot's own audio cannot trip its own barge.
decision.impact: Eliminates the bot-replies-to-itself loop. Cost: during TTS the user's words are not submitted to the LLM until they explicitly barge (cuts TTS, opens gate) or TTS ends naturally + cooldown expires. This matches normal human conversational turn-taking and prevents the failure mode. POST_TTS_COOLDOWN_S exposed as env knob for hardware tuning.

decision.title: Self-reply loop fixed at the audio layer via speexdsp AEC, not at the text or time layer
decision.summary: Text-content echo guard (D-015) and hard time gate on finals (D-016) were both downstream fixes — they tried to detect echo AFTER Deepgram had already transcribed it. Both leave failure modes (text hallucinations slip the heuristic; time gate prevents legitimate user interruption). The correct layer is audio: we know exactly what the bot is playing (the PCM we just handed to sounddevice), so we can run an adaptive filter against the mic input and subtract the bot's voice BEFORE Deepgram ever sees it. Backend: pyaec (ctypes bindings around speexdsp's AEC + preprocessor — same algorithm used by FreeSWITCH/Asterisk/SIP, battle-tested for 20+ years, prebuilt Windows/macOS/Linux wheels). Pure-numpy block-NLMS fallback retained for environments without pyaec. Measured ~20 dB ERLE after convergence on a synthetic round-trip test — bot audio is attenuated by 10x in the mic stream that reaches Deepgram. Time gate kept as a safety belt for the first few frames of convergence; with AEC working it's mostly redundant.
decision.impact: Loop dissolves at the source. Deepgram receives only the residual (real user speech + small AEC error). Crosstalk no longer needs to be defended downstream. The human can speak at any moment — including over the bot — and be heard, because the bot's voice has already been subtracted. No new compile-time dependencies on Windows (pyaec ships a wheel). Numpy fallback ensures graceful degrade.

decision.title: Speex preprocessor off by default; conversational behavior surfaces as named env knobs
decision.summary: After AEC was wired in, the user reported they could not interrupt and continue (their post-barge speech was lost). Root cause hypothesis: pyaec.Aec(enable_preprocess=True) wraps the AEC with speex's noise-suppress + AGC + VAD. The preprocessor's noise estimator learns the noise floor across frames; during a long bot turn that floor incorporates the AEC residual (small leftover echo), and after a barge the preprocessor keeps suppressing the user's voice because it sits within the learned floor. Decision: default AEC_PREPROCESS=0 so we get pure echo cancellation with no aggressive noise gating. Downstream STT (Deepgram) has its own VAD and tolerates real ambient noise. Operators can flip AEC_PREPROCESS=1 if they want the speex preprocessor back. Also: CROSSTALK_MIN_WORDS default lowered 3→1 — under MIN_WORDS=3, the most natural barge replies ('no', 'stop', 'wait') would never reach the LLM. Conversational behavior (barge sensitivity, speculation timing, cooldown) is now documented as a named env-knob surface in README so operators can tune for their environment without code changes.
decision.impact: User can now interrupt and continue — the post-barge continuation reaches the LLM. Pure AEC + Deepgram VAD is the new default audio pipeline; the speex preprocessor remains available as an opt-in for environments with heavy ambient noise. Conversational tuning section in README turns 'feels off' user feedback into a concrete dial.

decision.title: Speaker classification: pure-numpy MFCC+F0+centroid for V1; ONNX learned embeddings as documented upgrade path
decision.summary: Three-tier roadmap. Tier 0 (shipped): pure-numpy 29-dim feature vector — 13 MFCC + 13 delta-MFCC + F0 + spectral centroid + ZCR, cosine compare against enrolled user/bot templates. Zero new dependencies, ~1ms per inference, ~85-90 percent accuracy on a closed-set 2-speaker task. Auto-enrolls on first turn for each speaker; user template cached at ~/.conduit/voiceprint_user.npy. Tier 1 (documented, not yet built): convert a pretrained ECAPA-TDNN or x-vector model to ONNX, ship model file plus onnxruntime — ~30-50 MB total, ~256-dim learned embeddings, ~95-98 percent EER, no torch dep. Same extract_features/classify interface so it's a drop-in. Tier 2 (future): distilled int8 CNN at ~5-15 MB. Decision: ship Tier 0 first, observe whether residual misclassifications matter, only escalate when measured.
decision.impact: Adds a speaker-aware override to the barge logic without adding a 500 MB torch dependency. The 29-dim feature space and cosine-similarity gate is the same scaffolding used by every speaker-ID system; replacing handcrafted features with learned embeddings is a localized swap, not an architectural change. SPEAKER_MARGIN_THRESHOLD env knob lets operators tune confidence-vs-responsiveness without code changes. Classifier biases 'allow barge' when uncertain — the human must always be able to interrupt.

decision.title: Benchmark before claiming numbers; report what we measured not what we hoped
decision.summary: README previously claimed 85-90 percent classifier accuracy citing literature; benchmark harness reveals 80 percent on a synthetic-voice corpus (5 user clips + 5 bot clips, formant-synth, 2 false acceptances of bot-as-user). Synthetic sinusoidal voices are adversarial for MFCC features because they have less spectral diversity than real speech; real human + ElevenLabs TTS pairs should separate more cleanly. Decision: report measured numbers + disclose the synthetic-vs-real gap rather than echo literature claims. Benchmark harness accepts real wav files via --user/--bot so operators can produce environment-specific numbers. Also: AEC_PREPROCESS env knob defaulted back to ON — benchmark showed cleaned signal with large transients (max abs 32681 vs echo max 1600) when preprocess was disabled. The earlier 'preprocess off' default was a wrong fix for the post-barge gating issue; that issue was the gate-reclose state-machine bug (E6, fixed in 54c9bf6) and does not require disabling preprocess.
decision.impact: Stops the README from over-promising. Future tier upgrades (ONNX ECAPA-TDNN, distilled int8 model) are compared against the SAME harness on the SAME synthetic corpus plus operator-supplied real recordings — apples-to-apples size/accuracy tradeoff visible at decision time, not retroactively. AEC default restored to working state.
